name: io.github.amitshukla2308/hyperretrieval
title: HyperRetrieval
description: Codebase intelligence platform exposed as 15 MCP tools — precise search, graph navigation, blast-radius analysis, change prediction, PR review, activity-weighted re-ranking, criticality scoring, auto-generated guardrail docs, and AI-code provenance consumption. Built for AI coding agents working in large, long-lived codebases. Compliance-ready for EU AI Act (August 2026), SOX, and PCI-DSS audit evidence.

environmentVariables.name: ARTIFACT_DIR
environmentVariables.description: Directory containing graph_with_summaries.json, vectors.lance, cochange_index.json, criticality_index.json, guardrails/

environmentVariables.name: EMBED_SERVER_URL
environmentVariables.description: URL of the embedding inference server (default: http://localhost:8001)

environmentVariables.name: HR_GUARD_PATH
environmentVariables.description: Path to the Guard prototype for pattern checks (optional)

highlights: Blast Radius v2 with activity-weighted reranking (+322% recall@10)
AI-code provenance consumption (Git AI / Agent Blame / Tabnine)
Compliance-ready: EU AI Act (Aug 2026), SOX, PCI-DSS
submission_blockers: pip package 'hyperretrieval' not yet PUBLISHED to PyPI (packaging VERIFIED 2026-04-17 c15 — pip install -e . works, 3 entry scripts install, serve imports; needs PyPI token + upload)
HR currently needs an ARTIFACT_DIR pre-built from build/ pipeline — registry should surface this as a setup step, not a silent failure
Amit's OK on external submission (inverted permission list requires no-OK, but this touches personal-account repo visibility)
io.github.amitshukla2308/hyperretrieval.license: Apache-2.0 (already at repo root LICENSE — 190 lines, verified 2026-04-17)
roadmap_to_submission: 1. Package HR as `pip install hyperretrieval` (pyproject.toml exists, need PyPI token)
2. Add `hr-serve` CLI entrypoint that starts both embed + mcp servers with one command
3. Ship a tiny 'demo_artifact/' in the pip package so first-run doesn't need build/ pipeline
4. Submit to official MCP Registry via the publish API at registry.modelcontextprotocol.io
